mode_reason: Pipeline health green (all 8 agents producing within cadence). FUSE green (all 5 checked files match GitHub). Auth OK (200). No urgent Mode 1/2 issues. Mode 3 selected for deep cost engineering analysis of churn-and-burn wind-down readiness and decider efficiency.

modes_checked.pipeline_health: green — all 8 agents producing. Poller 4h ago (12h cadence), analyst/curmudgeon minutes ago (30min cadence), decider 1h ago (1h cadence), integrity ran today, social last ran yesterday (daily), workspace-sync 3h ago (4h cadence), tinker this run.
modes_checked.infrastructure: green — all 5 FUSE-checked files match GitHub (wins.json, sections.json, digest-reviews.js, generate-html.js, index.html). Auth 200. Previous generate-html.js staleness appears RESOLVED.
modes_checked.cost_engineering: PROP-005 (decider pre-flight) still STILL_BROKEN. Churn-and-burn active with queue depth 2 — wind-down imminent but auto-restore has a COVERAGE GAP (see findings).
modes_checked.proposals: 1 self-fix pending (decider.md stale schedule descriptions). PROP-005 pending human application.

findings.description: CHURN-AND-BURN AUTO-RESTORE COVERAGE GAP: When the priority queue drains, the decider Step E auto-restores only the curmudgeon schedule (30min → 4h) and flips mode to BAU. However, the analyst (currently every 30min, Opus) and decider (currently every 1h, Opus) are ALSO on accelerated churn-and-burn schedules that were set manually. Nothing auto-restores these. When the queue empties (depth currently 2), the curmudgeon will drop to 4h BAU but the analyst and decider will remain at 30min and 1h respectively — burning Opus tokens at full churn-and-burn rate with minimal work to process.
findings.evidence: Decider Step E only calls update_scheduled_task on dome-curmudgeon (grep confirms 0 mentions of dome-analyst or dome-decider in decider.md). Analyst cron: */30. Decider cron: 0 * (hourly). No auto-restore mechanism exists for either.

suggested_fix.replace: Add dome-analyst restore to every 2h and dome-decider restore to every 4h alongside the curmudgeon restore
suggested_fix.rationale: All three Opus agents should auto-restore to BAU cadences when churn-and-burn ends. Without this, the pipeline will burn ~6x the normal Opus budget on the analyst alone (30min vs 2h) after the queue drains.

findings.description: OWNERSHIP MISALIGNMENT: 4 files listed as git-owned in CLAUDE.md and workspace-sync.md OWNED_BY_GIT but MISSING from build.js OWNERSHIP object: data/predictions.json, docs/llms.txt, docs/sitemap.xml, docs/robots.txt. Consequence: build.js publish does NOT copy these files git→workspace. workspace-sync correctly blocks pushing them workspace→git, but they never get refreshed from git either — creating a one-way staleness trap.
findings.evidence: grep for each file in build.js OWNERSHIP returns no match. CLAUDE.md line 49 lists predictions.json, line 51 lists docs/llms.txt,sitemap.xml,robots.txt. workspace-sync OWNED_BY_GIT array includes all 4.

suggested_fix.find: Add 4 missing entries to OWNERSHIP object
suggested_fix.rationale: Restore three-way consistency (CLAUDE.md ↔ build.js ↔ workspace-sync.md) per Phase 1 rule.

findings.description: 3 additional unclassified resources found: (1) data/kill-shots.json — exists in data/ but not in OWNERSHIP, CLAUDE.md, or workspace-sync.md. (2) monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/ — referenced in CLAUDE.md and decider.md Priority 1b but not in build.js OWNERSHIP. (3) monitor/external-reports/ — referenced in decider.md Priority 2 but not in build.js OWNERSHIP or CLAUDE.md file ownership table.
findings.evidence: ls data/ shows kill-shots.json. grep issue-proposals build.js → no match. grep external-reports build.js → no match.

suggested_fix.replace: Add: 'data/kill-shots.json': 'git', 'monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/': 'append_only', 'monitor/external-reports/': 'append_only'. Also add kill-shots.json and issue-proposals/ to CLAUDE.md git-owned and append-only lists respectively.
suggested_fix.rationale: Unclassified files can cause silent ownership violations.

findings.description: Decider.md lines 12-14 have stale schedule descriptions: Poller listed as 'every 4h' (actual: every 12h), Curmudgeon listed as 'every 10min' (actual: every 30min in churn-and-burn, 4h BAU).
findings.evidence: decider.md line 12: 'Poller (every 4h)' vs cron '0 */12 * * *'. Line 14: 'Curmudgeon (every 10min)' vs cron '*/30 * * * *'.

suggested_fix.find:
- **Poller** (every 4h): Dome site changes
- **Analyst** (every 30min): Deep scientific analysis, new WIN entries, expansions
- **Curmudgeon** (every 10min): Adversarial self-review
suggested_fix.replace:
- **Poller** (every 12h): Dome site changes
- **Analyst** (every 30min churn / 2h BAU): Deep scientific analysis, new WIN entries, expansions
- **Curmudgeon** (every 30min churn / 4h BAU): Adversarial self-review
suggested_fix.rationale: Stale schedule info in the decider prompt means the decider may make wrong assumptions about upstream freshness.

previous_followup.evidence: grep -q 'Pre-flight Work Check' decider.md → not found. PROP-005.verification_pattern returns STILL_BROKEN. 5th consecutive report flagging this.

previous_followup.finding: Stale schedule descriptions in decider.md lines 12-14
previous_followup.evidence: decider.md still shows 'Poller (every 4h)' and 'Curmudgeon (every 10min)'. Deferred from 3+ reports. Self-fixable — will do on next Mode 4 run.

FUSE md5 check: all 5 files (wins.json, sections.json, digest-reviews.js, generate-html.js, index.html) match GitHub. The persistent staleness that affected AUDIT-001 verification has resolved — likely via a build.js publish sync run.
Closed in previous report. CLAUDE.md at 156 lines.

open_issue_health.aged_out: All 13 issues are 10+ days old (created ~2026-04-01). 3 major issues among them.
open_issue_health.repeated_deferrals: Several issues have been open since project inception without patches. However, most are large-scope items (predictions catalog, SHA-256 forensics, category proposals) that require multiple agent cycles.
open_issue_health.missing_patches: Not deeply audited this run (Mode 3 focus).

agent_efficiency.recommendation: PROP-005 (pre-flight gate) would save ~700 lines of reference reads per no-op run. 5th report flagging. Additionally, auto-restore to 4h BAU when churn-and-burn ends is critical.
Currently processing predictions and expansions. 64/68 expansions complete. Once prediction intake finishes, 30min cadence will produce mostly no-ops. No auto-restore to 2h exists.
Priority queue depth 2. Auto-restore to 4h BAU exists and will fire when queue empties. This is the ONLY agent with auto-restore — see major finding.
All recent runs report 'quiet'. 305-line prompt with no dispatcher. Candidate for dispatcher conversion, but Sonnet-daily is already cheap. Lower priority than Opus agent fixes.

churn_and_burn_status.auto_restore_coverage: PARTIAL — only curmudgeon has auto-restore. Analyst and decider schedules are manual churn-and-burn overrides with NO auto-restore mechanism.
churn_and_burn_status.estimated_churn_remaining: Queue depth 2 at 30min cadence = likely 1-2 hours until queue drains. Then curmudgeon auto-restores but analyst+decider stay fast.
churn_and_burn_status.cost_risk: After queue drains, analyst (Opus every 30min) will run 4x more than BAU with minimal work. Decider (Opus every 1h) will run 4x more than BAU with 50% no-op rate. Combined: roughly 8x normal Opus spend on two agents with mostly empty runs.

dispatcher_conversion_status.note: All Opus agents are converted or excluded. Remaining unconverted agents run on cheap models. Focus shifts to: (1) implementing PROP-005, (2) fixing churn-and-burn auto-restore gap, (3) schedule hygiene.

data/kill-shots.json exists but is not listed in CLAUDE.md file ownership table

Monitoring Pipeline table shows BAU schedules (analyst 2h, decider 4h) which are correct as defaults, but doesn't note current churn-and-burn overrides. Acceptable since these are temporary.

claude_md_audit.performance_assessment: At 156 lines / ~2717 tokens, CLAUDE.md is lean and well-structured after PROP-006 refactor. No content identified for extraction to reference/. All sections are read by multiple agents. No bloat detected.

recommendations.description: URGENT: Add auto-restore for analyst and decider schedules to the decider's Step E4 churn-and-burn queue-empty handler. Without this, when the priority queue drains (likely within hours), the curmudgeon drops to 4h BAU but analyst stays at 30min Opus and decider stays at 1h Opus — burning ~8x normal Opus budget. Options: (A) extend Step E4 to restore dome-analyst to 2h and dome-decider to 4h alongside curmudgeon, or (B) manually restore schedules now since queue depth is only 2.
Apply PROP-005 (decider pre-flight work check). 5th consecutive report. Would save ~700 lines of reference reads per no-op run. PROP file has exact find/replace text ready to apply.

Fix OWNERSHIP misalignment: add data/predictions.json, docs/llms.txt, docs/sitemap.xml, docs/robots.txt to build.js OWNERSHIP. Add data/kill-shots.json, monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/, monitor/external-reports/ as well. Routes through decider since build.js is a git-owned file.
Fix decider.md stale schedule descriptions (lines 12-14). Poller: 4h→12h. Curmudgeon: 10min→30min churn/4h BAU.

notes_to_future_tinker: The churn-and-burn auto-restore gap is the highest-priority finding. If the queue drains before it's fixed, manually check analyst and decider schedules. PROP-005 has been pending for 5 reports — consider whether to write a PROP-007 that bundles PROP-005 + the auto-restore fix + the schedule description fix into a single decider.md update to reduce implementation friction. The FUSE staleness of generate-html.js that persisted across 3+ reports is now resolved.
